items.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.5
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active in early 2026 with several major model releases and strategic announcements. Most recently, the company released Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6, with Opus 4.6 positioned as the industry-leading model for agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance tasks. Sonnet 4.6 provides a balanced option combining speed and intelligence, supporting a 1M token context window in beta and improved agentic search performance. Claude Haiku 3 is being deprecated wit

items.title: Exclusive: Anthropic 'Mythos' AI model representing 'step change' - Fortune
items.author: fortune.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a significant start to 2026 with two major model releases in February. Claude Opus 4.6 launched on February 5 as the most capable model yet, featuring a 1-million-token context window, top performance on the Finance Agent benchmark, and a 14.5-hour task completion horizon for long-running autonomous work. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed on February 17 as the new default for most users, closing the gap with Opus on coding, document comprehension, and office tasks. Anthropic also intr

items.title: Claude Developer Platform - Claude API Docs (Release Notes)
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available as the latest balanced model, offering improved agentic search performance with a 1M token context window (beta) and extended thinking support. The max_tokens cap has been raised to 300k on the Message Batches API for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 via the output-300k-2026-03-24 beta header. Data residency controls were introduced, allowing inference_geo parameter specification for US-only in

items.title: How AI assistance impacts the formation of coding skills
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been actively publishing research in early 2026 across several areas. Their Anthropic Economic Index series tracks how Claude is being used in real-world economic contexts: the January 2026 report introduced new 'economic primitives' metrics based on November 2025 interactions, and the March 2026 report titled 'Learning Curves' studied February 2026 Claude usage—coinciding with the Claude Opus 4.6 release period. A separate labor market impacts paper (March 2026) presented a new fr

items.title: Anthropic accidentally leaked details of a new AI model that poses unprecedented cybersecurity risks | Fortune
items.author: fortune.com
items.snippet: Anthropic's @AnthropicAI account has been active with several major announcements in late March–April 2026. The biggest story is the accidental leak of a new model called Claude Mythos, described by Anthropic as 'a step change' in capabilities and 'the most capable we've built to date.' A draft blog post was inadvertently left in a publicly searchable data store, revealing the model's name and noting Anthropic believes it poses unprecedented cybersecurity risks. Anthropic attributed the exposure
